title: Smoky Fluffy Japanese Eggs Omelette
description: A light, smoky Japanese-style fluffy omelette, perfect as a post-workout snack. The airy texture and subtle smoke flavor pair wonderfully with a protein boost.

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: Whole milk or unsweetened almond milk

instructions: Separate the eggs into a bowl: keep yolks in one bowl, whites in another.
Whisk the yolks with milk, salt, pepper, and liquid smoke until smooth.
Whisk the whites until soft peaks form, then fold them into the yolk mixture gently to preserve air.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add the vegetable oil, swirling to coat.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet, spreading evenly; cook uncovered until the edges set but the top remains slightly runny (~3‑4 minutes).
Fold the omelette in thirds and continue cooking until the top is fully set but still moist (~2 minutes).
Slide the omelette onto a plate, drizzle with soy sauce, and sprinkle chopped scallions on top.
Serve immediately while the omelette is still fluffy and warm.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
